summary.title: Demo: All Widgets
summary.subtitle: Every widget type — live examples and the JSON behind them

items.text: Displays numbers with optional comparison to a previous value
Supports units, trend arrows (up / down / flat), and a source label
Use for: performance benchmarks, test results, delivery stats, bug counts

detailBlocks.content:
```json
{
  "type": "metric",
  "title": "Example — all options",
  "metrics": [
    { "label": "Response Time (P95)", "value": 48,      "previousValue": 115,   "unit": "ms",  "trend": "down", "source": "Datadog" },
    { "label": "Error Rate",          "value": "0.4%", "previousValue": "1.2%",                 "trend": "down" },
    { "label": "Uptime",              "value": "99.97%",                                         "trend": "up" },
    { "label": "Endpoints Migrated",  "value": "12/12",                                          "trend": "up" }
  ]
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `label` | string | Metric name |
| `value` | string / number | Current value |
| `previousValue` | string / number | Shows a before/after comparison |
| `unit` | string | Appended to the value, e.g. `ms`, `KB` |
| `trend` | `up` / `down` / `flat` | Arrow indicator |
| `source` | string | Attribution label (e.g. Datadog, Mixpanel) |

items.text: Renders any GitHub-flavored markdown: headings, bold, italic, lists, tables, code blocks
Ideal for technical write-ups, decision rationale, approach notes, runbooks
Use a title to give context; omit it for inline narrative between other widgets

detailBlocks.title: Example — rich text
detailBlocks.content:
### Why we chose Elasticsearch

We evaluated **Typesense**, **Meilisearch**, and **Elasticsearch**. Elasticsearch won on three dimensions:

1. **Scale** — handles our 148k product catalog with room to grow
2. **Faceting** — native support for multi-level category filtering
3. **Team familiarity** — two engineers have prior production experience with it

*Typesense* was the runner-up. We'll revisit if hosting costs become a concern.

```json
{
  "type": "markdown",
  "title": "Example — rich text",
  "content": "### Why we chose Elasticsearch\n\nWe evaluated **Typesense**, **Meilisearch**, and **Elasticsearch**...\n\n1. **Scale** — handles our 148k product catalog\n2. **Faceting** — native multi-level filtering\n3. **Team familiarity** — two engineers have prior experience\n"
}
```

Use `\n` for newlines inside the `content` string. Standard markdown syntax works: `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `# Headings`, `- lists`, `` `inline code` ``.

```json
{
  "type": "markdown",
  "title": "Example — table",
  "content": "| Metric | Before | After | Delta |\n|--------|--------|-------|-------|\n| Bundle Size | 372 KB | 245 KB | -34% |\n| LCP        | 3.2s   | 1.8s  | -44% |"
}
```

GFM table syntax: header row, separator row (`|---|`), then data rows. Use `\n` between rows.

detailBlocks.title: Example — code block

detailBlocks.content:
Fenced code blocks use triple backticks with an optional language hint for syntax highlighting:

    ```typescript
    const x = 1;
    ```

In JSON, the backticks don't need escaping — only the inner quotes and newlines do:

```json
{
  "type": "markdown",
  "content": "```typescript\nasync function check() {\n  const count = await redis.zcard(key);\n}\n```"
}
```

items.text: Shows team member activity: commits, PRs opened/merged, lines added/removed
All stat fields are optional — show only what's meaningful for your team
Use for: sprint activity summaries, recognizing contributors, highlighting ownership

detailBlocks.content:
```json
{
  "type": "contributor-list",
  "title": "Example — full stats",
  "contributors": [
    { "name": "Sarah Chen",    "username": "schen",   "commits": 45, "prsOpened": 8, "prsMerged": 7, "linesAdded": 3420, "linesRemoved": 1205 },
    { "name": "Priya Patel",   "username": "ppatel",  "commits": 52, "prsOpened": 7, "prsMerged": 6, "linesAdded": 4100, "linesRemoved": 890 },
    { "name": "Marcus Rivera", "username": "mrivera", "commits": 38, "prsOpened": 5, "prsMerged": 5, "linesAdded": 2850, "linesRemoved": 1640 }
  ]
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `name` | string | Display name |
| `username` | string | Used for avatar lookup and `@mention` display |
| `commits` | number | |
| `prsOpened` | number | |
| `prsMerged` | number | |
| `linesAdded` | number | |
| `linesRemoved` | number | |

All stat fields are optional — omit any you don't track.

items.text: Three chart types: line, area, bar
Supports multiple series with custom colors
Use for: trends over a sprint, before/after comparisons, distribution data
Lists links to PRs, commits, issues, docs, or any external URL
Each link can have a type badge, repo label, and description
Use for: related pull requests, design docs, runbooks, demo recordings

links.label: feat: OAuth2 PKCE login flow
links.description: Complete rewrite of login using PKCE with refresh token rotation

links.label: fix: token invalidation race on Safari
Auth Overhaul RFC
links.description: Original design doc with threat model and token strategy

detailBlocks.content:
```json
{
  "type": "link-list",
  "title": "Related Work",
  "links": [
    {
      "label": "feat: OAuth2 PKCE login flow",
      "url": "https://github.com/acme/atlas-web/pull/142",
      "repo": "atlas-web",
      "type": "pr",
      "description": "Complete rewrite of login using PKCE"
    },
    {
      "label": "Auth Overhaul RFC",
      "url": "https://docs.acme.internal/rfcs/auth",
      "type": "doc"
    }
  ]
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `label` | string | Display text for the link |
| `url` | string | Where the link goes |
| `type` | `pr` / `issue` / `commit` / `doc` / `external` | Badge shown next to the label |
| `repo` | string | Repo attribution label |
| `description` | string | Optional subtitle shown below the label |

All fields except `label` and `url` are optional.

items.text: Embeds a video from YouTube, Vimeo, or any URL supported by the browser
Optional title and description for context
Use for: demo recordings, walkthroughs, incident retrospectives, onboarding clips

detailBlocks.title: Example — demo recording
detailBlocks.description: Walkthrough of the new 3-step onboarding experience (recorded Mar 24)

detailBlocks.content:
```json
{
  "type": "video",
  "title": "Example — demo recording",
  "description": "Walkthrough of the new 3-step onboarding experience",
  "url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ"
}
```

| Field | Type | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|
| `url` | string | YouTube, Vimeo, or direct video URL |
| `title` | string | Optional heading above the player |
| `description` | string | Optional caption below the title |

`url` is the only required field. YouTube `watch?v=` URLs are automatically converted to embeds.
